name: TinyCode-python
license_evidence: HF API cardData.license = 'mit' (verified via https://huggingface.co/api/models/blueapple8259/TinyCode-python). MIT is permissive. No standalone LICENSE file in repo tree, but the model card's explicit license field declares MIT. Tokenizer (GPT-2-style BPE) ships with the model.
arch: LlamaForCausalLM, hidden=128, layers=4, heads=16, kv_heads=4, vocab=52000, UNTIED embeddings, fp32
quant: int8 / int4 (symmetric group-wise, group=64, per-group fp16 scale; embed_tokens AND lm_head both quantized since untied; 1-D norms fp16)
runtime: transformers.js (q4f16) WASM in-browser, or llama.cpp/llama2.c int4 dequant-on-load. NOT built to WASM here (runtime path noted only).
honest_assessment: int4 = 12.3 MB on tape, well under the dream/working tiers — comfortably fits. Note the model is huge-vocab (52000) on a tiny hidden (128) with intermediate_size 11008, so embeddings + a single fat MLP dominate; int4 rel err 13% is acceptable for a code-completion toy. int8 (28.4 MB) is the higher-fidelity option if a tier allows it. Source is fp32 (4 bytes/param) which is why raw is 121 MB for only 30M params.
